use std::fs::File;
use std::io::Write;

// BAD: ? after partial side effects without cleanup
fn write_and_backup(data: &[u8], path: &str, backup_path: &str) -> Result<(), Error> {
    let mut file = File::create(path)?;
    file.write_all(data)?;
    file.flush()?;

    // If this fails, we've already written to `path` but have no backup
    let mut backup = File::create(backup_path)?;
    backup.write_all(data)?;
    backup.flush()?;

    Ok(())
}

fn transfer_funds(from: &mut Account, to: &mut Account, amount: u64) -> Result<(), Error> {
    from.balance -= amount;
    from.save()?;

    // If this fails, funds are deducted but not credited
    to.balance += amount;
    to.save()?;

    Ok(())
}
